verses.text: The three friends fall silent, since Job will not yield, and young Elihu's anger rises — against Job for justifying himself, and against them for condemning without an answer. A new voice prepares to speak. Sometimes the old arguments must die before truth can be heard afresh.

The three friends fall silent, since Job will not yield, and young Elihu's anger rises — against Job for justifying himself, and against them for condemning without an answer. A new voice prepares to speak. Sometimes the old arguments must die before truth can be heard afresh.

The three friends fall silent, since Job will not yield, and young Elihu's anger rises — against Job for justifying himself, and against them for condemning without an answer. A new voice prepares to speak. Sometimes the old arguments must die before truth can be heard afresh.

Elihu had waited in deference to his elders, but now their failure stirs him to speak. Respect held him back; truth now moves him forward. There is a time to defer to age and a time when faithfulness must speak regardless.

Elihu had waited in deference to his elders, but now their failure stirs him to speak. Respect held him back; truth now moves him forward. There is a time to defer to age and a time when faithfulness must speak regardless.
'I am young, and ye are very old,' Elihu admits, yet 'great men are not always wise.' Years do not guarantee understanding. Wisdom is the gift of God's Spirit, not merely the fruit of grey hairs.

'I am young, and ye are very old,' Elihu admits, yet 'great men are not always wise.' Years do not guarantee understanding. Wisdom is the gift of God's Spirit, not merely the fruit of grey hairs.
He has weighed their words and found none who truly answered Job; now he will give his own. He claims a fair hearing. A patient listener earns the right to speak, having first sought to understand.

He has weighed their words and found none who truly answered Job; now he will give his own. He claims a fair hearing. A patient listener earns the right to speak, having first sought to understand.
The friends stand amazed, with nothing more to say, so Elihu will take his turn. Their silence opens the door. When old counsel runs dry, God may raise up another to speak His truth.

The friends stand amazed, with nothing more to say, so Elihu will take his turn. Their silence opens the door. When old counsel runs dry, God may raise up another to speak His truth.

'I am full of matter,' he says; the spirit within constrains him like wine seeking vent, and he will flatter no one. He must speak, and speak honestly. The servant of truth fears God too much to fawn on any man.
